title: fix: drop FORWARD_QUERY from generation_event datasource
author: voodoohop

gist.headline: Skipping the heavy lifting
gist.blurb: We optimized our observability deployments to skip massive data copies. Tinybird updates are now lightning fast and metadata-only.
gist.summary: Removed the FORWARD_QUERY block from the generation_event datasource to prevent unnecessary 30 GiB data backfills during Tinybird deployments.
gist.impact: Deployments to our observability stack are now significantly faster and more efficient, using metadata-only ALTER TABLE operations.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee and the monitor robot bypassing a massive, heavy stack of server data boxes, opting for a lightweight, glowing floppy disk instead to represent the metadata-only update.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot flying happily past a massive, dusty stack of server boxes, carrying a single glowing floppy disk. A chunky pixel robot with a CRT monitor head gives a thumbs up. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Nostalgic but beautiful, emotionally warm.
